code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object value (remove surrounding quotes if present)
    def clean_object(obj):
        if obj and obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            return obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    # Find the main subject (the written work)
    main_subject = None
    if triples:
        main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Comprehensive templates for all predicates
    templates = {
        "author": f"{main_subject} was written by {{object}}",
        "isbnNumber": f"its ISBN number is {{object}}",
        "followedBy": f"it was followed by {{object}}",
        "precededBy": f"it was preceded by {{object}}",
        "language": f"it is written in {{object}}",
        "mediaType": f"its media type is {{object}}",
        "numberOfPages": f"it has {{object}} pages",
        "country": f"it was published in {{object}}",
        "publisher": f"its publisher is {{object}}",
        "genre": f"it belongs to the {{object}} genre",
        "literaryGenre": f"its literary genre is {{object}}",
        "releaseDate": f"it was released on {{object}}",
        "birthDate": f"the author was born on {{object}}",
        "almaMater": f"the author attended {{object}}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": f"the author's doctoral advisor was {{object}}",
        "nationality": f"the author is {{object}}",
        "residence": f"the author resides in {{object}}",
        "codenCode": f"its CODEN code is {{object}}",
        "issnNumber": f"its ISSN number is {{object}}",
        "LCCN_number": f"its Library of Congress Control Number is {{object}}",
        "abbreviation": f"it is abbreviated as {{object}}",
        "academicDiscipline": f"it covers the {{object}} discipline",
        "firstPublicationYear": f"it was first published in {{object}}",
        "editor": f"its editor is {{object}}",
        "impactFactor": f"it has an impact factor of {{object}}",
        "oclcNumber": f"its OCLC number is {{object}}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": f"its Library of Congress classification is {{object}}",
        "eissnNumber": f"its electronic ISSN is {{object}}",
        "frequency": f"it is published {{object}}",
        "headquarter": f"its headquarters are in {{object}}",
        "leader": f"its leader is {{object}}",
        "birthPlace": f"the author was born in {{object}}",
        "affiliation": f"it is affiliated with {{object}}",
        "city": f"it is located in {{object}}",
        "president": f"its president is {{object}}",
        "state": f"it is in the state of {{object}}",
        "spokenIn": f"{{subject}} is spoken in {{object}}",
        "influencedBy": f"the author was influenced by {{object}}",
        "family": f"it belongs to the {{object}} family",
        "ethnicGroup": f"the predominant ethnic group is {{object}}",
        "largestCity": f"its largest city is {{object}}",
        "location": f"it is located in {{object}}",
        "deathPlace": f"the author died in {{object}}",
        "notableWork": f"the author's notable work is {{object}}",
        "parentCompany": f"its parent company is {{object}}",
        "regionServed": f"it serves the {{object}} region",
        "founder": f"it was founded by {{object}}",
        "capital": f"the capital is {{object}}",
        "demonym": f"the people are known as {{object}}",
        "leaderTitle": f"the leader's title is {{object}}",
        "nickname": f"it is nicknamed {{object}}",
        "genus": f"it belongs to the {{object}} genus",
        "cityServed": f"it serves {{object}}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        obj = clean_object(t.object)
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine clauses into one coherent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    elif len(clauses) == 2:
        sentence = f"{clauses[0]}, and {clauses[1]}"
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
